tasks.title: [CMS] Implement Boards API routes with stage management

Ensure all routes are protected by the admin auth middleware. Interact with the `boards` and `stages` Firestore collections.

Write integration tests using a mocked Firebase Admin SDK to verify board CRUD operations and project filtering. Specifically test the stage reordering logic to ensure Firestore batch writes correctly update the `order` fields without duplication or gaps. Test bulk task operations by seeding mock tasks, executing the endpoints, and verifying the resulting task states. Verify that requests lacking a valid admin token return 401/403 errors.

tasks.title: Fix Storage Rules isAdmin() Custom Claims Mismatch
tasks.description: Resolve the mismatch between Firestore user roles and Firebase Auth custom claims for admin users to fix broken brand image uploads.
tasks.details: Create a Cloud Function triggered on Firestore user document writes. If `role === 'admin'`, execute `admin.auth().setCustomUserClaims(uid, { admin: true })`. Alternatively, update `storage.rules` to validate admin status via a Firestore lookup instead of `request.auth.token.admin`. Ensure the chosen method securely gates `/brands/**` paths.
tasks.testStrategy: Log in as an admin user. Attempt to upload an image to the `/brands/` path. Verify the upload succeeds. Attempt the same as a non-admin user and verify it fails with a permission denied error.

subtasks.title: Implement Chosen Solution: Cloud Function or Direct Firestore Lookup in Rules
subtasks.description: Decide on and implement one of the two proposed solutions. Either create a Cloud Function to sync a user's admin role from their Firestore document to their Auth custom claims, or update the storage security rules to perform a direct Firestore document lookup to verify admin status.
subtasks.details: If implementing the Cloud Function, create an `onUpdate` trigger for the `users/{userId}` collection. The function will check if the `role` field has been set to 'admin' and then use `admin.auth().setCustomUserClaims()` to apply the `{ admin: true }` claim. If updating storage rules, modify the `isAdmin()` helper function to use `get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'admin'`. This avoids reliance on custom claims.
subtasks.testStrategy: For the Cloud Function, unit tests can be written to mock Firestore triggers and verify that `setCustomUserClaims` is called with the correct parameters. For the storage rule change, testing will be performed in the subsequent task.

subtasks.title: Write and Execute Security Rule Tests Using Firebase Emulator

subtasks.details: Using the `@firebase/rules-unit-testing` library, set up tests for the `storage.rules`. The suite should include test cases for an admin user attempting to write a file to `/brands/test-brand/logo.png` (should succeed) and a non-admin user attempting the same action (should be denied). The tests must cover the specific implementation from the previous task.
subtasks.testStrategy: Execute the test suite via a script (e.g., `npm test`). The test runner should report success for all defined test cases, confirming that the security logic correctly distinguishes between admin and non-admin users.

subtasks.title: Deploy Changes and Perform Manual End-to-End Verification
subtasks.description: Deploy the new Cloud Function or updated storage rules to the live Firebase environment. After deployment, conduct manual end-to-end testing to confirm that the brand image upload functionality is fixed for admins and remains secure against non-admin access.
subtasks.details: Use the Firebase CLI to deploy the changes (`firebase deploy --only functions,storage`). Log into the application using a real admin account and verify that uploading a brand image works as expected. Then, log in with a standard non-admin user account and confirm that any attempt to upload a brand image is blocked and results in a permission error.
subtasks.testStrategy: Manual verification in the production environment. Log in as an admin, upload a brand image, and check Firebase Storage to confirm its presence. Log in as a non-admin, attempt the upload, and check the browser's network tab and console for a 403 Forbidden error from Firebase Storage.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break this task into three subtasks: 1. Implement the chosen solution (either the Cloud Function to sync claims or the updated storage rules with Firestore lookups). 2. Write and execute security rule tests using the Firebase Emulator to verify admin-only access. 3. Deploy the changes and perform a manual end-to-end test with real admin and non-admin accounts.

tasks.title: Feature: Implement Direct File Upload to Project Drive
tasks.description: Integrate the existing `FileUpload` component into the Project Files Hub to allow users to drag-and-drop files for direct upload to the current project's Google Drive folder.
tasks.details: In `ProjectFilesPage.tsx`, add the `FileUpload` component. Pass the current `projectId` to it, which will then be used by the underlying `useDriveFiles` hook to ensure uploads are sent to the correct, project-scoped API endpoint. The upload functionality should only be active when the 'Drive' or 'All' source is selected in the sidebar.
tasks.testStrategy: Navigate to the project files hub. Drag a file onto the upload zone. Verify the upload progress appears and completes successfully. Check the project's folder in Google Drive to confirm the file is there. Test that attempting to upload while a non-Drive source is selected is disabled or handled gracefully.
tasks.expansionPrompt: This task is a straightforward integration of an existing component and does not require further expansion. The work involves placing the component and passing the correct props.

tasks.title: Integration: Add 'Files' Tool Card to ToolsPage
tasks.description: Add a new tool card for 'Files' on the `ToolsPage`, displaying live stats such as the number of Drive files, in-app attachments, and external links for the project.
tasks.details: In `pages/ToolsPage.tsx`, add a new entry to the tools array for 'Files'. The `href` should dynamically point to `/projects/${project.id}/files`. To get the stats, use the same data sources: `useDriveFiles({ projectId })` for a lightweight file count and filter the `DataContext` collections (`tasks`, `projectLinks`, etc.) by `projectId` to get the counts for attachments and links. Memoize these calculations for performance.
tasks.testStrategy: Navigate to the `ToolsPage` for a project. Verify the 'Files' card is present. Check that the stats displayed are accurate. Click the card and confirm it navigates to the correct project files hub URL. Verify stats update when new files or links are added.

subtasks.title: UI: Add Static 'Files' Tool Card to ToolsPage
subtasks.description: Create the static UI for the new 'Files' tool card on the `ToolsPage`. This includes adding the card with its icon, title, and a static description, ensuring it routes correctly to the project's files hub.
subtasks.details: In `pages/ToolsPage.tsx`, add a new entry to the tools array for 'Files'. Set the title, icon, and a placeholder description. The `href` property must be dynamically constructed to point to `/projects/${project.id}/files`. The stats display can be hardcoded or show a loading state initially.
subtasks.testStrategy: Navigate to the `ToolsPage` for a project. Verify the 'Files' card is present and correctly styled. Click the card and confirm it navigates to the correct project files hub URL.

subtasks.title: Data: Fetch and Display Live Stats on 'Files' Tool Card
subtasks.description: Implement the logic to fetch, calculate, and display the live statistics for the 'Files' tool card. This includes the count of Drive files, in-app attachments, and external links associated with the project.
subtasks.details: In `pages/ToolsPage.tsx`, use the `useDriveFiles({ projectId })` hook for the Drive file count. Access the `DataContext` to filter and count `tasks` with attachments and `projectLinks` for the current project. Memoize these calculations for performance and pass the aggregated stats to the card component.
subtasks.testStrategy: On the `ToolsPage`, verify that the 'Files' card displays accurate counts for a project with known data. Add a new file, attachment, or link and confirm the stats update correctly on the card. Check for loading and zero-states.

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down the task of adding the 'Files' tool card. Subtasks should be: 1. Add the static UI for the new card to the `ToolsPage` with correct routing. 2. Implement the data fetching and aggregation logic to calculate and display the live stats (file counts, link counts) on the card.

tasks.title: Navigation: Update Main App Sidebar
tasks.description: Modify the main application sidebar's 'Files' link to be context-aware. It should navigate to the active project's file hub if a project is selected, or to the global files page otherwise.
tasks.details:
In the main sidebar component, use the `ActiveProjectContext` to get the currently active project. The 'Files' navigation link's `to` or `href` prop should be determined conditionally: `activeProject ? 
`/projects/${activeProject.id}/files` : '/files'`. This ensures users are always taken to the most relevant files view.
tasks.testStrategy: With no project selected, click the 'Files' link in the sidebar and verify it goes to `/files`. Select a project, then click the 'Files' link again and verify it now navigates to `/projects/{projectId}/files`. Switch projects and confirm the link updates accordingly.
tasks.expansionPrompt: This task is a small, targeted change to a single component and does not require further expansion. It involves adding a context hook and a ternary operator to a link's prop.
